close_reason: All crates share version.workspace = true, so secretx-core version coupling is managed. SecretError is already in the API surface via SigningBackend and error returns; the re-export is ergonomic, not additional coupling.
description:
lib.rs line 49: 'pub use secretx_core::SecretError;'

This re-export makes SecretError part of secretx-signature's public API. Every public item that returns or accepts SecretError is now tied to the exact secretx-core major version. If secretx-core bumps its major version (e.g. for breaking changes to SecretError fields, variant types, or trait impls), secretx-signature must also bump — and callers who depend on both crates could face diamond-dependency conflicts.

The doc at lines 44-47 acknowledges the signature/secretx-core version coupling, but does not mention this re-export as an additional lock. Callers are not warned that importing SecretError from secretx-signature is equivalent to depending on secretx-core directly.

If secretx-signature is intended to be the stable adapter boundary, it should define its own error type (or newtype wrap SecretError) rather than re-exporting the upstream type.
title: pub re-export of SecretError locks secretx-core major version into public API
